title: Zliide In-Store Ordering App
description: Designed an in-store ordering app increasing physical fashion stores monthly store revenue up by additional 6% by reducing out-of-stock situations.
problem: Zliide is a technology company that seeks to reinvent the fashion industry by making physical retail stores more digital with solutions and features such as assisted-checkout, in-store ordering and informative dashboards. The existing in-store ordering experience left shoppers stranded when an item was out of stock, sending revenue out the door. The task was to improve the current user experience of the in-store ordering app.
challenge: The task was to improve the current user experience of the in-store ordering app, with a particular focus on reducing out-of-stock situations and the lost sales they caused.
solution: The concept was enhanced through feedback gathered from brand stakeholders, store employees, and store customers. The new flow let shoppers order out-of-stock items on the spot, and new Figma design system components and tokens were added while others were updated to support it.

process.body: Gathered feedback from brand stakeholders, store employees, and store customers to map where the existing in-store ordering flow broke down — most acutely when an item was out of stock.
Reworked the core in-store ordering journey so a shopper facing an out-of-stock item could order it on the spot, rather than leaving empty-handed.
Added new Figma design system components and tokens, and updated existing ones, so the new patterns were reusable and consistent across the product.
Tested the new solution against the old experience. It was selected as the preferred choice by 100% of participants and measured a 21.3% lift in user satisfaction.

outcomes.label: monthly store revenue
outcomes.note: Additional revenue per physical store from reduced out-of-stock situations.

outcomes.label: preferred in user testing
outcomes.note: Every participant chose the new solution over the existing experience.

outcomes.label: user satisfaction
outcomes.note: Measured improvement from the redesigned ordering flow.
